edges.description: Abnormal anomalous drainage pathways can be narrowed or obstructed, raising pulmonary venous pressure and worsening postnatal compromise.
Orphanet records atrial septal defect as a frequent associated cardiac phenotype in total anomalous pulmonary venous return.
Obstructed pulmonary venous return worsens systemic desaturation and respiratory compromise.
Orphanet records respiratory distress as a very frequent clinical manifestation of total anomalous pulmonary venous return.
Orphanet records pulmonary arterial hypertension as a frequent phenotype in total anomalous pulmonary venous return.
Orphanet records tachycardia as a frequent cardiopulmonary manifestation of total anomalous pulmonary venous return.
Orphanet records exertional dyspnea as a frequent respiratory phenotype in total anomalous pulmonary venous return.
Orphanet records fatigue as a frequent systemic manifestation of total anomalous pulmonary venous return.
Orphanet records paroxysmal dyspnea as a frequent respiratory phenotype in total anomalous pulmonary venous return.
Orphanet records right ventricular failure as an occasional cardiac phenotype in total anomalous pulmonary venous return.
Orphanet records cardiomegaly as an occasional cardiac phenotype in total anomalous pulmonary venous return.

nodes.description: Total anomalous drainage returns oxygenated pulmonary venous blood to the right-sided circulation, creating obligatory right-to-left mixing at the atrial level and abnormal pulmonary blood flow.

evidence.reference_title: Surgical repair of total anomalous pulmonary venous connection.
evidence.snippet: The diagnosis of total anomalous pulmonary venous connection (TAPVC) is made when all four pulmonary veins drain anomalously to the right atrium or to a tributary of the systemic veins.

evidence.reference_title: Computed tomography features of supracardiac total anomalous pulmonary venous connection in an infant.
evidence.snippet: TAPVC is caused by nonfusion of pulmonary venous confluence with LA, thus, the oxygenated pulmonary blood is redirected to right heart and pumped once again to the lungs

nodes.description: Atrial septal defect is a frequent associated cardiac phenotype reported in total anomalous pulmonary venous return.
Cardiomegaly is an occasional cardiac phenotype in TAPVR.
Systemic desaturation results from obligatory mixing of pulmonary and systemic venous blood.

evidence.reference_title: Surgical repair of total anomalous pulmonary venous connection.
evidence.snippet: The clinical presentation is different if the pulmonary venous drainage is unobstructed (heart failure, mild cyanosis) or obstructed (respiratory failure, severe heart failure).

nodes.description: Exertional dyspnea is a frequent respiratory manifestation in TAPVR.
Fatigue is a frequent systemic manifestation reported in TAPVR.
Paroxysmal dyspnea is a frequent respiratory manifestation in TAPVR.
Increased pulmonary blood flow and venous obstruction can raise pulmonary arterial pressure.

evidence.reference_title: Computed tomography features of supracardiac total anomalous pulmonary venous connection in an infant.
evidence.snippet: Based on its type and degree of pulmonary venous obstruction, TAPVC may result in pulmonary hypertension and congestive heart failure

nodes.description: Narrowed or obstructed anomalous venous pathways can elevate pulmonary venous pressure, worsening cyanosis, pulmonary edema, and pulmonary hypertension.

evidence.reference_title: Surgical repair of total anomalous pulmonary venous connection.
evidence.snippet: The clinical presentation is different if the pulmonary venous drainage is unobstructed (heart failure, mild cyanosis) or obstructed (respiratory failure, severe heart failure).

evidence.reference_title: Assessing the risk of reoperation for mild pulmonary vein obstruction post-TAPVC repair: a retrospective cohort study.
evidence.snippet: Our results suggest that a pre-discharge echocardiography Doppler velocity threshold of 1.2 m/s could serve as a critical predictor for reoperation, emphasizing the need for targeted follow-up strategies for at-risk patients.

nodes.description: Obstructed TAPVR can present with severe respiratory compromise due to pulmonary venous hypertension and pulmonary edema.

evidence.reference_title: Surgical repair of total anomalous pulmonary venous connection.
evidence.snippet: The clinical presentation is different if the pulmonary venous drainage is unobstructed (heart failure, mild cyanosis) or obstructed (respiratory failure, severe heart failure).

nodes.description: Right ventricular failure is an occasional cardiac phenotype in TAPVR.
Surgical redirection of pulmonary venous drainage to the left atrium is the definitive treatment, with urgent repair required when venous obstruction is present.

evidence.reference_title: Surgical repair of total anomalous pulmonary venous connection.
evidence.snippet: Obstructed TAPVC requires urgent surgical intervention, whereas unobstructed TAPVC can be dealt with electively; although this is usually operated on once the diagnosis is made.

evidence.reference_title: Total anomalous pulmonary venous connection in 80 patients: Primary sutureless repair and outcomes.
evidence.snippet: The primary sutureless technique may eliminate the differences between subtypes while decrease the postoperative PVO rate, which makes it applicable in any subtypes of TAPVC.

nodes.description: Tachycardia is a frequent cardiopulmonary manifestation in TAPVR.
All pulmonary veins drain anomalously rather than connecting normally to the left atrium.

evidence.reference_title: Surgical repair of total anomalous pulmonary venous connection.
evidence.snippet: The diagnosis of total anomalous pulmonary venous connection (TAPVC) is made when all four pulmonary veins drain anomalously to the right atrium or to a tributary of the systemic veins.
